failure_scenarios.trigger_condition: Any follow-up request (POST /api/run-followup) that successfully generates a new synthesis
failure_scenarios.evidence: run_followup_stream uses state.previous_synthesis (the PREVIOUS turn's answer) as the 'response' field in the neuro/learn POST body, instead of state.final_solution.core_solution (the CURRENT turn's generated answer).
clear_cache() deletes disk files but never touches the module-level _MEMORY_CACHE dict. Subsequent requests may still return stale cached results from memory.

failure_scenarios.trigger_condition: Concurrent calls to AuthManager.generate_key() and AuthManager.authenticate()
failure_scenarios.evidence: generate_key() mutates self._keys without acquiring self._lock, while authenticate(), revoke_key(), and list_keys() all acquire the lock. Under threaded execution this is a data race.

root_cause_analyses.proximate_cause: Line ~807 uses state.previous_synthesis as the neuro/learn response payload
root_cause_analyses.root_cause: Developer assumed previous_synthesis would be updated by the pipeline, but the pipeline only writes to state.final_solution
contributing_factors: No unit test covers the neuro persistence payload
Ambiguous field naming: 'previous_synthesis' sounds like it should hold the current synthesis
execution_path: run_stream executes pipeline, writes new answer to state.final_solution
After stream completes, neuro/learn POST sends state.previous_synthesis (stale)

root_cause_analyses.proximate_cause: Function only iterates over CACHE_DIR.glob('*.json') and never touches _MEMORY_CACHE
root_cause_analyses.root_cause: In-memory cache layer was added after disk cache; clear_cache was not updated to handle both layers
contributing_factors: No test for clear_cache endpoint exists
_MEMORY_CACHE is a module-level mutable global managed by a separate module (cache.py)
execution_path: Next identical request hits _MEMORY_CACHE and returns stale data

root_cause_analyses.proximate_cause: Line 142 does 'self._keys[key_hash] = api_key' outside any lock
root_cause_analyses.root_cause: generate_key was implemented as a synchronous method before the asyncio.Lock was added to other AuthManager methods; it was never updated
contributing_factors: No production callers of generate_key exist in the current codebase
Inconsistent API design: all other mutating methods are async and use the lock
execution_path: Method mutates self._keys dict without serialization
Concurrent authenticate() coroutine may observe partial dict state

transitive_callers: Admin key management endpoints (not yet implemented)

selected_fix.description: Use state.final_solution.core_solution when available, else fall back to state.previous_synthesis

patches.rationale: Minimal one-line change that fixes the root cause. Preserves backward compatibility by falling back to previous_synthesis when final_solution is None (pipeline failure).
patches.patch_diff:
--- a/src/reasoner/api/__init__.py
+++ b/src/reasoner/api/__init__.py
@@ -804,7 +804,7 @@ async def run_followup_stream(req: FollowupRequest) -> AsyncGenerator[str, None]
                 'http://127.0.0.1:50001/neuro/learn',
                 json={
                     'prompt': req.question,
-                    'response': state.previous_synthesis,
+                    'response': (state.final_solution.core_solution if state.final_solution else state.previous_synthesis),
                     'agent_id': req.conversation_id,
                     'metadata': {
                         'turn_number': state.turn_number,

rollback_plan.revert_method: git revert safe — single line change
rollback_triggers: neuro service rejects new payload shape

attacks.attack: final_solution exists but core_solution is empty string
attacks.details: Empty string is a valid pipeline output and should be persisted as-is
Graceful fallback to previous_synthesis preserves existing behavior

regression_tests.description: Verify neuro persist prefers final_solution over previous_synthesis
regression_tests.preconditions: PipelineState with both previous_synthesis and final_solution populated

regression_tests.description: Verify graceful fallback when final_solution is None

patches.rationale: Single line addition that completes the cache invalidation contract. No side effects beyond the intended behavior.
patches.patch_diff:
--- a/src/reasoner/api/__init__.py
+++ b/src/reasoner/api/__init__.py
@@ -1034,6 +1034,7 @@ async def clear_cache():
             cleared += 1
         except OSError:
             pass
+    _MEMORY_CACHE.clear()
     return {'cleared': cleared}

rollback_plan.revert_method: git revert safe — single line addition
monitoring: Cache hit rate after admin cache clears
rollback_triggers: Unexpected memory cache misses after clear

attacks.details: In asyncio, dict.clear() has no await points and cannot be interleaved
dict.clear() is O(n) but n=256 is trivial

regression_tests.description: Verify clear_cache invalidates both disk and memory
regression_tests.preconditions: _MEMORY_CACHE seeded with test data; disk file created
regression_tests.failure_behavior_without_patch: Disk file deleted but _MEMORY_CACHE still contains data

selected_fix.description: Make generate_key async and wrap dict mutation in self._lock

patches.rationale: Changes method signature from def to async def for consistency with the rest of AuthManager. No production callers exist; tests were updated to await. This is the architecturally correct fix.
patches.patch_diff:
--- a/src/reasoner/auth.py
+++ b/src/reasoner/auth.py
@@ -95,7 +95,7 @@ class AuthManager:
         """Hash API key using SHA-256."""
         return hashlib.sha256(key.encode()).hexdigest()
 
-    def generate_key(
+    async def generate_key(
         self,
         name: str,
         expires_in_days: Optional[int] = None,
@@ -139,7 +139,8 @@ class AuthManager:
             created_by=created_by,
         )
 
-        self._keys[key_hash] = api_key
+        async with self._lock:
+            self._keys[key_hash] = api_key
         return raw_key

rollback_plan.revert_method: git revert safe — revert both auth.py and test_security.py
rollback_triggers: New async callers fail without await

attacks.details: Both operations now serialize via asyncio.Lock; dict corruption impossible
Regression test validates no lost keys or duplicates under concurrency

regression_tests.description: Verify generate_key is awaitable and stores key correctly
regression_tests.expected_behavior: Returns raw key string; key hash present in manager._keys

regression_tests.description: Verify concurrent generate_key calls are safe
regression_tests.input: Two batches of 50 concurrent generate_key calls
regression_tests.expected_behavior: All 100 keys stored uniquely, no duplicates, no missing
regression_tests.failure_behavior_without_patch: Possible dict corruption or lost keys under threaded execution

human_review_items: BUG-003 interface change: generate_key changed from def to async def. No production callers exist, but any external integrations using this method will need to add 'await'.
